title: Work function (W)
short: The minimum energy needed to free an electron from a metal's surface in the photoelectric effect.
full:
The work function W is the least energy required to remove an electron from the surface of a particular metal. It is a property of the metal, usually quoted in electron-volts (eV).

In the photoelectric effect a photon must supply at least W to eject an electron; any extra photon energy becomes the electron's kinetic energy, KE_max = hf − W. The threshold frequency f₀ = W/h is the lowest frequency of light that can free an electron from that metal.
